{\n376\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS headers '%s', got '%s'\", headers, actual)\n377\t\t\t\t}\n378\t\t\t\treturn nil\n379\t\t\t},\n380\t\t})\n381\t\treturn v\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// Assert runs all validations and asserts if any fail.\n385\t// This is the terminal method - call it to execute all validations.\n386\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) Assert() {\n387\t\tv.t.Helper()\n388\t\n389\t\t// Parse response once for all validations\n390\t\tif err := v.parse(); err != nil {\n391\t\t\tv.t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n392\t\t\treturn\n393\t\t}\n394\t\n395\t\t// Run all validations\n396\t\tfor _, exp := range v.expectations {\n397\t\t\tif err := exp.validate(v); err != nil {\n398\t\t\t\texp.failed = true\n399\t\t\t}\n400\t\t}\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t// parse parses the S3 error response.\n404\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) parse() error {\n405\t\tif v.s3Error != nil {\n406\t\t\treturn nil // Already parsed\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n410\t\t\treturn nil // Nothing to parse\n411\t\t}\n412\t\n413\t\tv.s3Error = &S3Error{}\n414\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(v.response.Body.Bytes(), v.s3Error); err != nil {\n415\t\t\treturn err\n416\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\treturn nil\n419\t}\n420\t\n421\t// =============================================================================\n422\t// SIMPLE VALIDATION HELPERS\n423\t// =============================================================================\n424\t\n425\t// VerifyStandardErrorResponse performs standard error response validation.\n426\t// This is a convenience function for common validation patterns.\n427\t//\n428\t// It verifies:\n429\t// - HTTP status code matches expected\n430\t// - Content-Type is \"application/xml\"\n431\t// - Response body is not empty\n432\t// - Error code matches expected\n433\t// - Error message is at least 15 characters\n434\t// - Response starts with XML declaration\n435\tfunc VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string, expectedStatus int) {\n436\t\tt.Helper()\n437\t\n438\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n439\t\t\tHTTPStatusCode(expectedStatus).\n440\t\t\tContentType(\"application/xml\").\n441\t\t\tBodyNotEmpty().\n442\t\t\tHasCode(expectedCode).\n443\t\t\tMessageMinLength(15).\n444\t\t\tHasXMLDeclaration().\n445\t\t\tAssert()\n446\t}\n447\t\n448\t// ParseErrorResponse parses an S3 error response from the response body.\n449\tfunc ParseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, body []byte) *S3Error {\n450\t\tt.Helper()\n451\t\n452\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n453\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n454\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n455\t\t}\n456\t\n457\t\treturn &s3Err\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\t// =============================================================================\n461\t// CORE VALIDATION HELPER FUNCTIONS\n462\t// =============================================================================\n463\t// These are standalone helper functions for validating HTTP error responses.\n464\t// They provide a simpler alternative to the fluent ErrorResponseValidator API.\n465\t// Each helper performs a single validation and can be used independently.\n466\t// =============================================================================\n467\t\n468\t// ValidateHTTPStatusCode validates the HTTP status code of an error response.\n469\t//\n470\t// This helper function checks that the response has the expected HTTP status code.\n471\t// It's useful for verifying that error responses return the correct status codes\n472\t// (e.g., 404 for NotFound, 403 for Forbidden, 400 for BadRequest).\n473\t//\n474\t// Example:\n475\t//   ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n476\tfunc ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n477\t\tt.Helper()\n478\t\n479\t\tif w.Code != expectedCode {\n480\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, w.Code)\n481\t\t}\n482\t}\n483\t\n484\t// ValidateContentType validates the Content-Type header of an error response.\n485\t//\n486\t// This helper function checks that the response has the expected Content-Type header.\n487\t// For S3-compatible error responses, this should typically be \"application/xml\".\n488\t// ValidateContentType is defined in content_type_validation.go with enhanced functionality.\n489\t\n490\t// ValidateErrorStructure validates that an error response has proper structure.\n491\t//\n492\t// This helper function checks that the error response contains:\n493\t// - A non-empty response body\n494\t// - A valid S3 Error XML structure with Code and Message fields\n495\t// - A non-empty error code\n496\t// - A non-empty error message\n497\t//\n498\t// This ensures the error response is well-formed and contains the required fields.\n499\t//\n500\t// Example:\n501\t//   ValidateErrorStructure(t, w)\n502\tfunc ValidateErrorStructure(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n503\t\tt.Helper()\n504\t\n505\t\t// Check response body is not empty\n506\t\tif w.Body.Len() == 0 {\n507\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response body is empty\")\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\t// Parse the error response\n511\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n512\t\n513\t\t// Validate error code is present\n514\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n515\t\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Code field\")\n516\t\t}\n517\t\n518\t\t// Validate error message is present\n519\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n520\t\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Message field\")\n521\t\t}\n522\t}\n523\t\n524\t// ValidateErrorCode validates the S3 error code in an error response.\n525\t//\n526\t// This helper function checks that the error response contains the expected S3 error code.\n527\t// Common S3 error codes include: NoSuchKey, AccessDenied, InvalidRequest, etc.\n528\t//\n529\t// Example:\n530\t//   ValidateErrorCode(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\")\n531\tfunc ValidateErrorCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n532\t\tt.Helper()\n533\t\n534\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n535\t\n536\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n537\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n538\t\t}\n539\t}\n540\t\n541\t// ValidateCORSHeaders validates that CORS headers are present on an error response.\n542\t//\n543\t// This helper function checks that the error response includes the required CORS headers:\n544\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n545\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n546\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n547\t//\n548\t// This ensures that error responses properly support cross-origin requests.\n549\t//\n550\t// Example:\n551\t//   ValidateCORSHeaders(t, w)\n552\tfunc ValidateCORSHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n553\t\tt.Helper()\n554\t\n555\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n556\t\torigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n557\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n558\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n559\t\t}\n560\t\n561\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n562\t\tmethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n563\t\tif methods == \"\" {\n564\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n565\t\t}\n566\t\n567\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n568\t\theaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n569\t\tif headers == \"\" {\n570\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n571\t\t}\n572\t}\n573\t\n574\t// ValidateCORSOrigin validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n575\t//\n576\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS origin.\n577\t// Common values are \"*\" (allow all origins) or a specific origin.\n578\t//\n579\t// Example:\n580\t//   ValidateCORSOrigin(t, w, \"*\")\n581\tfunc ValidateCORSOrigin(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedOrigin string) {\n582\t\tt.Helper()\n583\t\n584\t\tactualOrigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n585\t\tif actualOrigin != expectedOrigin {\n586\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", expectedOrigin, actualOrigin)\n587\t\t}\n588\t}\n589\t\n590\t// ValidateCORSMethods validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n591\t//\n592\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS methods.\n593\t// Common values include: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\"\n594\t//\n595\t// Example:\n596\t//   ValidateCORSMethods(t, w, \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\n597\tfunc ValidateCORSMethods(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedMethods string) {\n598\t\tt.Helper()\n599\t\n600\t\tactualMethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n601\t\tif actualMethods != expectedMethods {\n602\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", expectedMethods, actualMethods)\n603\t\t}\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// ValidateCORSAllowHeaders validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n607\t//\n608\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS headers.\n609\t// Common values include: \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\"\n610\t//\n611\t// Example:\n612\t//   ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t, w, \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n613\tfunc ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedHeaders string) {\n614\t\tt.Helper()\n615\t\n616\t\tactualHeaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n617\t\tif actualHeaders != expectedHeaders {\n618\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS allow-headers '%s', got '%s'\", expectedHeaders, actualHeaders)\n619\t\t}\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t// ValidateErrorMessageContains validates that the error message contains expected text.\n623\t//\n624\t// This helper function checks that the error response message contains the specified substring.\n625\t// This is useful for verifying that error messages are descriptive and helpful.\n626\t//\n627\t// Example:\n628\t//   ValidateErrorMessageContains(t, w, \"not found\")\n629\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedText string) {\n630\t\tt.Helper()\n631\t\n632\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n633\t\n634\t\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n635\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, s3Err.Message)\n636\t\t}\n637\t}\n638\t\n639\t// ValidateErrorMessageMinLength validates that the error message meets minimum length.\n640\t//\n641\t// This helper function checks that the error response message is at least the specified\n642\t// number of characters. This ensures error messages are sufficiently descriptive.\n643\t//\n644\t// Example:\n645\t//   ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t, w, 15)\n646\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, minLength int) {\n647\t\tt.Helper()\n648\t\n649\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n650\t\n651\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n652\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n653\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n654\t\t}\n655\t}\n656\t\n657\t// ValidateXMLDeclaration validates that the response starts with an XML declaration.\n658\t//\n659\t// This helper function checks that the error response begins with \"<?xml\" which indicates\n660\t// a proper XML declaration. S3 error responses should be valid XML documents.\n661\t//\n662\t// Example:\n663\t//   ValidateXMLDeclaration(t, w)\n664\tfunc ValidateXMLDeclaration(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n665\t\tt.Helper()\n666\t\n667\t\tbody := w.Body.String()\n668\t\n669\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n670\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected response to start with XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n671\t\t}\n672\t}\n673\t\n674\t// =============================================================================\n675\t// ERROR RESPONSE STRUCTURE VALIDATION\n676\t// =============================================================================\n677\t// These helpers provide comprehensive error response structure validation\n678\t// with support for optional field validation and detailed error reporting.\n679\t// =============================================================================\n680\t\n681\t// ErrorStructureValidationOptions configures error structure validation.\n682\ttype ErrorStructureValidationOptions struct {\n683\t\t// RequireCode ensures the error code field is present and non-empty\n684\t\tRequireCode bool\n685\t\t// RequireMessage ensures the error message field is present and non-empty\n686\t\tRequireMessage bool\n687\t\t// ExpectedCode checks that the error code matches the expected value\n688\t\tExpectedCode string\n689\t\t// MinMessageLength ensures the error message meets minimum length\n690\t\tMinMessageLength int\n691\t\t// MessageContains checks that the error message contains specific text\n692\t\tMessageContains string\n693\t\t// CustomFields validates additional custom fields in the error response\n694\t\tCustomFields map[string]string\n695\t}\n696\t\n697\t// DefaultValidationOptions returns default validation options.\n698\tfunc DefaultValidationOptions() ErrorStructureValidationOptions {\n699\t\treturn ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n700\t\t\tRequireCode:      true,\n701\t\t\tRequireMessage:   true,\n702\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n703\t\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n704\t\t}\n705\t}\n706\t\n707\t// ValidateErrorResponseStructure validates the structure of an error response.\n708\t//\n709\t// This helper function performs comprehensive validation of error response structure:\n710\t// - Validates response body is not empty\n711\t// - Validates error field exists (Code field)\n712\t// - Validates error message is present and non-empty\n713\t// - Supports optional field validation via options\n714\t//\n715\t// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n716\t// Note: This function does not call t.Error() to allow testing of invalid cases.\n717\t// Use AssertValidErrorResponseStructure() for assertion behavior.\n718\t//\n719\t// Parameters:\n720\t//   t: Testing instance for context\n721\t//   body: Response body as byte array\n722\t//   options: Validation options (use DefaultValidationOptions() for defaults)\n723\t//\n724\t// Example:\n725\t//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n726\t//   opts.RequireCode = true\n727\t//   opts.RequireMessage = true\n728\t//   opts.MinMessageLength = 15\n729\t//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody, opts)\n730\tfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n731\t\tt.Helper()\n732\t\n733\t\t// Check response body is not empty\n734\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n735\t\t\treturn false\n736\t\t}\n737\t\n738\t\t// Parse the error response\n739\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n740\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n741\t\t\treturn false\n742\t\t}\n743\t\n744\t\t// Track validation results\n745\t\tallValid := true\n746\t\n747\t\t// Validate error code field exists if required\n748\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n749\t\t\tallValid = false\n750\t\t}\n751\t\n752\t\t// Validate error message field exists if required\n753\t\tif options.RequireMessage {\n754\t\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n755\t\t\tif trimmedMsg == \"\" {\n756\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n757\t\t\t}\n758\t\t}\n759\t\n760\t\t// Validate expected code if specified\n761\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n762\t\t\tallValid = false\n763\t\t}\n764\t\n765\t\t// Validate minimum message length if specified\n766\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n767\t\t\t// Use trimmed message for length validation\n768\t\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n769\t\t\tif len(trimmedMsg) < options.MinMessageLength {\n770\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n771\t\t\t}\n772\t\t}\n773\t\n774\t\t// Validate message contains expected text if specified (case-insensitive)\n775\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" {\n776\t\t\tmessageLower := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n777\t\t\tkeywordLower := strings.ToLower(options.MessageContains)\n778\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(messageLower, keywordLower) {\n779\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n780\t\t\t}\n781\t\t}\n782\t\n783\t\t// Validate custom fields if specified\n784\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n785\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n786\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n787\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n788\t\t\t}\n789\t\t}\n790\t\n791\t\treturn allValid\n792\t}\n793\t\n794\t// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with defaults.\n795\t//\n796\t// This is a simplified version that uses default validation options.\n797\t// It validates:\n798\t// - Response body is not empty\n799\t// - Error code field exists and is non-empty\n800\t// - Error message field exists and is non-empty (min 10 chars)\n801\t//\n802\t// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n803\t//\n804\t// Example:\n805\t//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, responseBody)\n806\tfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t *testing.T, body []byte) bool {\n807\t\tt.Helper()\n808\t\treturn ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, DefaultValidationOptions())\n809\t}\n810\t\n811\t// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure validates error structure and asserts if invalid.\n812\t//\n813\t// This helper validates error response structure and fails the test if validation fails.\n814\t// Use this when you want test execution to stop on validation failure.\n815\t// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n816\t//\n817\t// Example:\n818\t//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody)\n819\tfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte) {\n820\t\tt.Helper()\n821\t\n822\t\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, body) {\n823\t\t\t// Provide detailed error message\n824\t\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n825\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n826\t\t\t}\n827\t\n828\t\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n829\t\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n830\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n831\t\t\t}\n832\t\n833\t\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n834\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n835\t\t\t}\n836\t\n837\t\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n838\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n839\t\t\t}\n840\t\n841\t\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < 10 {\n842\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least 10): %s\",\n843\t\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), s3Err.Message)\n844\t\t\t}\n845\t\n846\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n847\t\t}\n848\t}\n849\t\n850\t// AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions validates with custom options and asserts.\n851\t//\n852\t// This helper validates error response structure with custom options and fails the test\n853\t// if validation fails. Use this when you need specific validation requirements.\n854\t// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n855\t//\n856\t// Example:\n857\t//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n858\t//   opts.MinMessageLength = 20\n859\t//   opts.MessageContains = \"authentication\"\n860\t//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t, responseBody, opts)\n861\tfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) {\n862\t\tt.Helper()\n863\t\n864\t\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options) {\n865\t\t\t// Provide detailed error message based on options\n866\t\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n867\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n868\t\t\t}\n869\t\n870\t\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n871\t\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n872\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n873\t\t\t}\n874\t\n875\t\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n876\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n877\t\t\t}\n878\t\n879\t\t\tif options.RequireMessage && s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n880\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n881\t\t\t}\n882\t\n883\t\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n884\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected code '%s', got '%s'\",\n885\t\t\t\t\toptions.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n886\t\t\t}\n887\t\n888\t\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 && len(s3Err.Message) < options.MinMessageLength {\n889\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n890\t\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), options.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n891\t\t\t}\n892\t\n893\t\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, options.MessageContains) {\n894\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n895\t\t\t\t\toptions.MessageContains, s3Err.Message)\n896\t\t\t}\n897\t\n898\t\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n899\t\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n900\t\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n901\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected field '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\",\n902\t\t\t\t\t\tfield, expectedValue, actualValue)\n903\t\t\t\t}\n904\t\t\t}\n905\t\n906\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n907\t\t}\n908\t}\n909\t\n910\t// getXMLField extracts a field value from XML response body.\n911\t// This is a helper for validating custom fields in error responses.\n912\tfunc getXMLField(body []byte, field string) string {\n913\t\t// Simple XML field extraction using string search\n914\t\t// This is basic but works for common S3 error response structures\n915\t\topenTag := \"<\" + field + \">\"\n916\t\tcloseTag := \"</\" + field + \">\"\n917\t\n918\t\tbodyStr := string(body)\n919\t\topenIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr, openTag)\n920\t\tif openIdx == -1 {\n921\t\t\treturn \"\"\n922\t\t}\n923\t\n924\t\tcloseIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr[openIdx:], closeTag)\n925\t\tif closeIdx == -1 {\n926\t\t\treturn \"\"\n927\t\t}\n928\t\n929\t\treturn strings.TrimSpace(bodyStr[openIdx+len(openTag):openIdx+closeIdx])\n930\t}\n931\t\n932\t// =============================================================================\n933\t// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n934\t// =============================================================================\n935\t\n936\t// CreateTestRequest creates an HTTP request for testing.\n937\t//\n938\t// This helper centralizes request creation and ensures consistent test setup.\n939\t// It supports:\n940\t// - Custom HTTP methods and paths\n941\t// - Optional request body\n942\t// - Custom headers\n943\t// - Test server URL format\n944\tfunc CreateTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body io.Reader, headers map[string]string) *http.Request {\n945\t\tt.Helper()\n946\t\n947\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, body)\n948\t\n949\t\t// Set default headers for test requests\n950\t\tif headers == nil {\n951\t\t\theaders = make(map[string]string)\n952\t\t}\n953\t\n954\t\t// Set custom headers\n955\t\tfor key, value := range headers {\n956\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n957\t\t}\n958\t\n959\t\treturn req\n960\t}\n961\t\n962\t// CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest creates a properly authenticated test request.\n963\t//\n964\t// This helper creates a request with valid AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 authentication.\n965\t// It's useful for testing non-authentication errors (where auth should succeed).\n966\tfunc CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string) *http.Request {\n967\t\tt.Helper()\n968\t\n969\t\t// Use the working authentication helper from auth_integration_test.go\n970\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, method, path, \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n971\t}\n972\t\n973\t\n974\t// =============================================================================\n975\t// TEST SCENARIO STRUCTURES\n976\t// =============================================================================\n977\t\n978\t// ErrorScenario represents a testable error scenario.\n979\ttype ErrorScenario struct {\n980\t\t// Name is the scenario name for test reporting\n981\t\tName string\n982\t\n983\t\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n984\t\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n985\t\n986\t\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n987\t\tExpectedCode string\n988\t\n989\t\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n990\t\tExpectedStatus int\n991\t\n992\t\t// ExpectedKeywords are keywords that should appear in the error message\n993\t\tExpectedKeywords []string\n994\t\n995\t\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n996\t\tMinMessageLength int\n997\t\n998\t\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n999\t\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n1000\t\n1001\t\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n1002\t\tDescription string\n1003\t}\n1004\t\n1005\t// ErrorScenarioResult captures the result of running a scenario.\n1006\ttype ErrorScenarioResult struct {\n1007\t\tScenarioName string\n1008\t\tStatusCode   int\n1009\t\tErrorCode    string\n1010\t\tMessage      string\n1011\t\tDuration     time.Duration\n1012\t\tPassed       bool\n1013\t}\n1014\t\n1015\t// RunErrorScenario executes a single error scenario and returns the result.\n1016\tfunc RunErrorScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n1017\t\tt.Helper()\n1018\t\n1019\t\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n1020\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1021\t\n1022\t\t// Measure response time\n1023\t\tstart := time.Now()\n1024\t\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n1025\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n1026\t\n1027\t\t// Parse response\n1028\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n1029\t\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n1030\t\n1031\t\t// Build result\n1032\t\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n1033\t\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n1034\t\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n1035\t\t\tErrorCode: {\n\t\t\t\tv.t.Errorf(\"Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\t\tkeywords, ev.s3Error.Message)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// HasCORSHeaders verifies that CORS headers are present on the error response.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasCORSHeaders() *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"HasCORSHeaders\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\torigin := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tmethods := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\t\t\tif methods == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\theaders := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\t\t\tif headers == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSOrigin verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSOrigin(origin string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSOrigin\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\t\t\tif actual != origin {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", origin, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSMethods verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSMethods(methods string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSMethods\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\t\t\tif actual != methods {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", methods, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// CORSHeaders verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSHeaders(headers string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n\t\tname: \"CORSHeaders\",\n\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\t\t\tif actual != headers {\n\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS headers '%s', got '%s'\", headers, actual)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\treturn v\n}\n\n// Assert runs all validations and asserts if any fail.\n// This is the terminal method - call it to execute all validations.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) Assert() {\n\tv.t.Helper()\n\n\t// Parse response once for all validations\n\tif err := v.parse(); err != nil {\n\t\tv.t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Run all validations\n\tfor _, exp := range v.expectations {\n\t\tif err := exp.validate(v); err != nil {\n\t\t\texp.failed = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// parse parses the S3 error response.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) parse() error {\n\tif v.s3Error != nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Already parsed\n\t}\n\n\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil // Nothing to parse\n\t}\n\n\tv.s3Error = &S3Error{}\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(v.response.Body.Bytes(), v.s3Error); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SIMPLE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// VerifyStandardErrorResponse performs standard error response validation.\n// This is a convenience function for common validation patterns.\n//\n// It verifies:\n// - HTTP status code matches expected\n// - Content-Type is \"application/xml\"\n// - Response body is not empty\n// - Error code matches expected\n// - Error message is at least 15 characters\n// - Response starts with XML declaration\nfunc VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string, expectedStatus int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n\t\tHTTPStatusCode(expectedStatus).\n\t\tContentType(\"application/xml\").\n\t\tBodyNotEmpty().\n\t\tHasCode(expectedCode).\n\t\tMessageMinLength(15).\n\t\tHasXMLDeclaration().\n\t\tAssert()\n}\n\n// ParseErrorResponse parses an S3 error response from the response body.\nfunc ParseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, body []byte) *S3Error {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &s3Err\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE VALIDATION HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n// These are standalone helper functions for validating HTTP error responses.\n// They provide a simpler alternative to the fluent ErrorResponseValidator API.\n// Each helper performs a single validation and can be used independently.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateHTTPStatusCode validates the HTTP status code of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected HTTP status code.\n// It's useful for verifying that error responses return the correct status codes\n// (e.g., 404 for NotFound, 403 for Forbidden, 400 for BadRequest).\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t, w, 404)\nfunc ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif w.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, w.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateContentType validates the Content-Type header of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected Content-Type header.\n// For S3-compatible error responses, this should typically be \"application/xml\".\n// ValidateContentType is defined in content_type_validation.go with enhanced functionality.\n\n// ValidateErrorStructure validates that an error response has proper structure.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains:\n// - A non-empty response body\n// - A valid S3 Error XML structure with Code and Message fields\n// - A non-empty error code\n// - A non-empty error message\n//\n// This ensures the error response is well-formed and contains the required fields.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorStructure(t, w)\nfunc ValidateErrorStructure(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check response body is not empty\n\tif w.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the error response\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\t// Validate error code is present\n\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Code field\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error message is present\n\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Message field\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorCode validates the S3 error code in an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains the expected S3 error code.\n// Common S3 error codes include: NoSuchKey, AccessDenied, InvalidRequest, etc.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorCode(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc ValidateErrorCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSHeaders validates that CORS headers are present on an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response includes the required CORS headers:\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n//\n// This ensures that error responses properly support cross-origin requests.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSHeaders(t, w)\nfunc ValidateCORSHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n\torigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n\tmethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif methods == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n\theaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif headers == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSOrigin validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS origin.\n// Common values are \"*\" (allow all origins) or a specific origin.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSOrigin(t, w, \"*\")\nfunc ValidateCORSOrigin(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedOrigin string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualOrigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif actualOrigin != expectedOrigin {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", expectedOrigin, actualOrigin)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSMethods validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS methods.\n// Common values include: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\"\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSMethods(t, w, \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\nfunc ValidateCORSMethods(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedMethods string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualMethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif actualMethods != expectedMethods {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", expectedMethods, actualMethods)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSAllowHeaders validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS headers.\n// Common values include: \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\"\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t, w, \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\nfunc ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedHeaders string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualHeaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif actualHeaders != expectedHeaders {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS allow-headers '%s', got '%s'\", expectedHeaders, actualHeaders)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageContains validates that the error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message contains the specified substring.\n// This is useful for verifying that error messages are descriptive and helpful.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorMessageContains(t, w, \"not found\")\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageMinLength validates that the error message meets minimum length.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message is at least the specified\n// number of characters. This ensures error messages are sufficiently descriptive.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t, w, 15)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, minLength int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateXMLDeclaration validates that the response starts with an XML declaration.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response begins with \"<?xml\" which indicates\n// a proper XML declaration. S3 error responses should be valid XML documents.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateXMLDeclaration(t, w)\nfunc ValidateXMLDeclaration(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody := w.Body.String()\n\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected response to start with XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR RESPONSE STRUCTURE VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide comprehensive error response structure validation\n// with support for optional field validation and detailed error reporting.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorStructureValidationOptions configures error structure validation.\ntype ErrorStructureValidationOptions struct {\n\t// RequireCode ensures the error code field is present and non-empty\n\tRequireCode bool\n\t// RequireMessage ensures the error message field is present and non-empty\n\tRequireMessage bool\n\t// ExpectedCode checks that the error code matches the expected value\n\tExpectedCode string\n\t// MinMessageLength ensures the error message meets minimum length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\t// MessageContains checks that the error message contains specific text\n\tMessageContains string\n\t// CustomFields validates additional custom fields in the error response\n\tCustomFields map[string]string\n}\n\n// DefaultValidationOptions returns default validation options.\nfunc DefaultValidationOptions() ErrorStructureValidationOptions {\n\treturn ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n\t\tRequireCode:      true,\n\t\tRequireMessage:   true,\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructure validates the structure of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function performs comprehensive validation of error response structure:\n// - Validates response body is not empty\n// - Validates error field exists (Code field)\n// - Validates error message is present and non-empty\n// - Supports optional field validation via options\n//\n// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n// Note: This function does not call t.Error() to allow testing of invalid cases.\n// Use AssertValidErrorResponseStructure() for assertion behavior.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   t: Testing instance for context\n//   body: Response body as byte array\n//   options: Validation options (use DefaultValidationOptions() for defaults)\n//\n// Example:\n//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n//   opts.RequireCode = true\n//   opts.RequireMessage = true\n//   opts.MinMessageLength = 15\n//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody, opts)\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check response body is not empty\n\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the error response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Track validation results\n\tallValid := true\n\n\t// Validate error code field exists if required\n\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tallValid = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error message field exists if required\n\tif options.RequireMessage {\n\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tif trimmedMsg == \"\" {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate expected code if specified\n\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tallValid = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate minimum message length if specified\n\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n\t\t// Use trimmed message for length validation\n\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tif len(trimmedMsg) < options.MinMessageLength {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate message contains expected text if specified (case-insensitive)\n\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" {\n\t\tmessageLower := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tkeywordLower := strings.ToLower(options.MessageContains)\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(messageLower, keywordLower) {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate custom fields if specified\n\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn allValid\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with defaults.\n//\n// This is a simplified version that uses default validation options.\n// It validates:\n// - Response body is not empty\n// - Error code field exists and is non-empty\n// - Error message field exists and is non-empty (min 10 chars)\n//\n// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example:\n//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, responseBody)\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t *testing.T, body []byte) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\treturn ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, DefaultValidationOptions())\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure validates error structure and asserts if invalid.\n//\n// This helper validates error response structure and fails the test if validation fails.\n// Use this when you want test execution to stop on validation failure.\n// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n//\n// Example:\n//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, body) {\n\t\t// Provide detailed error message\n\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < 10 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least 10): %s\",\n\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions validates with custom options and asserts.\n//\n// This helper validates error response structure with custom options and fails the test\n// if validation fails. Use this when you need specific validation requirements.\n// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n//\n// Example:\n//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n//   opts.MinMessageLength = 20\n//   opts.MessageContains = \"authentication\"\n//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t, responseBody, opts)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options) {\n\t\t// Provide detailed error message based on options\n\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.RequireMessage && s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected code '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\toptions.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 && len(s3Err.Message) < options.MinMessageLength {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), options.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, options.MessageContains) {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\toptions.MessageContains, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected field '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfield, expectedValue, actualValue)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// getXMLField extracts a field value from XML response body.\n// This is a helper for validating custom fields in error responses.\nfunc getXMLField(body []byte, field string) string {\n\t// Simple XML field extraction using string search\n\t// This is basic but works for common S3 error response structures\n\topenTag := \"<\" + field + \">\"\n\tcloseTag := \"</\" + field + \">\"\n\n\tbodyStr := string(body)\n\topenIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr, openTag)\n\tif openIdx == -1 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tcloseIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr[openIdx:], closeTag)\n\tif closeIdx == -1 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.TrimSpace(bodyStr[openIdx+len(openTag):openIdx+closeIdx])\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CreateTestRequest creates an HTTP request for testing.\n//\n// This helper centralizes request creation and ensures consistent test setup.\n// It supports:\n// - Custom HTTP methods and paths\n// - Optional request body\n// - Custom headers\n// - Test server URL format\nfunc CreateTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body io.Reader, headers map[string]string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, body)\n\n\t// Set default headers for test requests\n\tif headers == nil {\n\t\theaders = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set custom headers\n\tfor key, value := range headers {\n\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest creates a properly authenticated test request.\n//\n// This helper creates a request with valid AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 authentication.\n// It's useful for testing non-authentication errors (where auth should succeed).\nfunc CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Use the working authentication helper from auth_integration_test.go\n\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, method, path, \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n}\n\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST SCENARIO STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorScenario represents a testable error scenario.\ntype ErrorScenario struct {\n\t// Name is the scenario name for test reporting\n\tName string\n\n\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n\tExpectedCode string\n\n\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n\tExpectedStatus int\n\n\t// ExpectedKeywords are keywords that should appear in the error message\n\tExpectedKeywords []string\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n\n\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n\tDescription string\n}\n\n// ErrorScenarioResult captures the result of running a scenario.\ntype ErrorScenarioResult struct {\n\tScenarioName string\n\tStatusCode   int\n\tErrorCode    string\n\tMessage      string\n\tDuration     time.Duration\n\tPassed       bool\n}\n\n// RunErrorScenario executes a single error scenario and returns the result.\nfunc RunErrorScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t// Measure response time\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\n\t// Parse response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n\n\t// Build result\n\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n\t\tDuration:     duration,\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate result\n\tresult.Passed = true\n\n\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = scenario.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check keywords if specified\n\tif len(scenario.ExpectedKeywords) > 0 {\n\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, keyword := range scenario.ExpectedKeywords {\n\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = =============================================================================\n// BASE TEST STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorTestSuite provides a base structure for error response test suites.\n//\n// This structure helps organize related error tests and provides\n// consistent reporting and validation across test types.\ntype ErrorTestSuite struct {\n\tt          *testing.T\n\tname       string\n\tfixture    *TestServerFixture\n\tscenarios  []ErrorScenario\n\tresults    []*ErrorScenarioResult\n}\n\n// NewErrorTestSuite creates a new error test suite.\nfunc NewErrorTestSuite(t *testing.T, name string) *ErrorTestSuite {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn &ErrorTestSuite{\n\t\tt:       t,\n\t\tname:    name,\n\t\tfixture: NewTestServer(t),\n\t\tresults: make([]*ErrorScenarioResult, 0),\n\t}\n}\n\n// AddScenario adds a test scenario to the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) AddScenario(scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorTestSuite {\n\tsuite.scenarios = append(suite.scenarios, scenario)\n\treturn suite\n}\n\n// Run executes all scenarios in the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) Run() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// RunSequentially executes all scenarios sequentially (not in parallel).\n// Use this when scenarios have dependencies or share state.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) RunSequentially() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Parallel() // Disable parallelism\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// ReportStatistics logs performance statistics for the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) ReportStatistics() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tif len(suite.results) == 0 {\n\t\tsuite.t.Log(\"No results to report\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar total, max, min time.Duration\n\tmin = 1 * time.Second // Initialize to high value\n\tpassed := 0\n\tfailed := 0\n\n\tfor _, result := range suite.results {\n\t\ttotal += result.Duration\n\t\tif result.Duration > max {\n\t\t\tmax = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Duration < min {\n\t\t\tmin = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Passed {\n\t\t\tpassed++\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfailed++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tavg := total / time.Duration(len(suite.results))\n\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"Error Test Suite: %s\", suite.name)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Total scenarios: %d\", len(suite.results))\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Passed: %d, Failed: %d\", passed, failed)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Average response time: %v\", avg)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Min response time: %v\", min)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Max response time: %v\", max)\n}\n\n// the minimum of two integers.\nfunc min(a, b int) int {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// MeasureRequestTime measures the time to execute a request.\n// Returns the duration and response recorder.\nfunc MeasureRequestTime(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) (time.Duration, *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\treturn duration, w\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DOCUMENTATION
